channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kelowna Welcomes Memorial Cup, Boosting Local Economy and Tourism
item.description: The Memorial Cup has officially arrived in Kelowna, marking the start of a national junior hockey tournament that is set to energize the city. The trophy was ceremonially welcomed by the Westbank Firs...

item.title: DStv offers discounted decoders ahead of Fifa World Cup 2026
item.description: As the Fifa World Cup 2026 draws near, excitement is building among South African football fans eager to follow Bafana Bafana’s journey. In response, DStv has introduced its lowest-ever prices for ent...

item.title: Uganda Restricts Transport and Border Movement with DR Congo
item.description: Uganda imechukua hatua kali ya kudhibiti usafiri na harakati kwenye mpaka wake na Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC). Serikali ya Uganda imesitisha huduma za feri kwenye Mto Semliki, ambao ni muhimu k...

item.title: Exploring Iconic Mid-Century Modern Home Interiors
item.description: Mid-Century Modern (MCM) house interiors, prominent in the 1950s and 1960s, marked a transformative shift in residential design. This style emphasized simplicity, functional layout, and organic forms,...

item.title: Senate Committee Reviews Deportation of Pakistani Nationals from UAE
item.description: ISLAMABAD: Parliamentary Affairs Minister Tariq Fazal Chaudhry informed the Senate on Thursday that approximately 3,494 Pakistani nationals were deported from the UAE between January and April 2026. H...

item.title: Sonko Confirms NEDP Will Use Democratic Process for Kwale Leadership Appointments
item.description: National Economic Development Party (NEDP) leader Mike Mbuvi Sonko has reassured Kenyans that the party remains committed to fairness, transparency, and inclusivity, especially in Kwale County. Speaki...

item.title: Tanzania calls East African countries to cooperate in safeguarding Lake Victoria
item.description: Tanzania is calling on all East African countries sharing Lake Victoria to strengthen cooperation for the protection and sustainable management of Africa’s largest freshwater lake. Prime Minister Dr. ...

item.title: Anne Hathaway clarifies rumors about plastic surgery and discusses public perception
item.description: In a recent interview with Elle, Anne Hathaway, 43, spoke candidly about a circulating rumor suggesting she had undergone plastic surgery. The actress emphasized that much of the public tends to assum...

item.title: Calgary resident critically injured after being hit by CTrain
item.description: In Calgary, police report that a person has been struck and trapped under a CTrain, resulting in life-threatening injuries. Emergency responders arrived promptly at the scene to provide immediate medi...

item.title: Why Financial Advisors Are Facing Silent Client Disengagement
item.description: A growing trend of clients 'ghosting' financial advisors has emerged in Canada, reflecting broader shifts in digital communication and societal norms. Research Co.'s 2023 survey indicates that over ha...

item.title: Portraits of Juliette Gréco Highlighting Her Iconic Style and Influence in Post-War France
item.description: Juliette Gréco (1927–2020) emerged as a central figure of post-war French culture, captivating audiences with her deep, husky voice and expressive interpretations of chanson. Her career began in the s...

item.title: US to Deploy 5,000 More Troops to Poland Amid NATO Tensions
item.description: US President Donald Trump has announced that an additional 5,000 American troops will be deployed to Poland, citing his close relationship with Poland’s newly elected conservative president, Karol Naw...

item.title: GOP Frustration Grows as Trump Pushes Controversial Spending and Demands in Congress
item.description: Republican leaders on Capitol Hill are increasingly frustrated with former President Donald Trump as his demands and initiatives alienate key members of his party. During a panel discussion on MSNBC, ...

item.title: New Robotics Lab Brings Coding, AI and Problem-Solving Skills to Soweto Learners
item.description: Learners in Soweto are now being offered exciting opportunities in coding, robotics, and artificial intelligence following the launch of a new robotics lab at Siyabonga Secondary School on 21 May 2026...

item.title: CBN Holds Interest Rate Amid Moderate Inflation and Strong Forex Position
item.description: The Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) don retain the Monetary Policy Rate (MPR) at 26.50% following its 305th Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting held on May 19–20, 2026. The decision dey influenced m...

item.title: Nicolas Torre Emphasizes Police Duty to Implement All Warrants Amid Dela Rosa Manhunt
item.description: Former Philippine National Police chief and current MMDA General Manager, Nicolas Torre III, emphasized that police officers cannot selectively enforce arrest warrants, especially during the ongoing g...

item.title: Jackie Moggridge: Trailblazing Female Aviator and WWII Ferry Pilot
item.description: Jackie Moggridge (1922–2004), born Dolores Theresa Sorour in South Africa, became one of the most remarkable female aviators of the 20th century. Renaming herself after her hockey hero, she began flyi...

item.title: Nigeria’s Ports Record Significant Growth Following Maritime Reforms
item.description: Nigeria maritime sector don dey show better growth for di first quarter of 2026 following the Federal Government reforms, infrastructural upgrades for ports, and increase for export activities. Di gov...

item.title: SpaceX Delays Starship V3 Launch Due to Last-Minute Technical Issue
item.description: SpaceX postponed the first launch of its third-generation Starship rocket from its Starbase facility in Texas, originally scheduled for May 21, 2026. The countdown was halted just under 40 seconds bef...

item.title: Mashatile affirms 1913 as the legal basis for land restitution, rejects 1652 cut-off
item.description: Deputy President Paul Mashatile has clarified that the South African government will not adopt 1652 as a cut-off date for land reform and restitution. Speaking in the National Assembly, Mashatile emph...
